title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Atlanta Braves in extra innings, falling to an 18-26 season record.
Braves' Mike Yastrzemski, Carl Yastrzemski's grandson, hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning, his seventh career walk-off.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs but had a strong outing, giving up five hits over five innings.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs interested in Patriots reunion, but A.J. Brown trade complicates possibilities.
Patriots likely to acquire wide receiver A.J. Brown, impacting cap space and Diggs's role.
New England offense improving; Diggs return would be unlikely with current financial outlook.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police are searching for Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, a 15-year-old boy last seen Tuesday afternoon near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester.
LaGrant-Gomes is described as a Black male, 5 feet 8 inches tall, with a thin build, brown eyes, and black hair.
Police encourage anyone with information to call 911 or contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or submit tips anonymously.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, arrested for allegedly shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man on May 10 in Dorchester.
The woman was hospitalized with life-threatening injuries while the other man sustained non-life-threatening wounds, per police.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder and unlawful possession of a firearm, awaiting a dangerousness hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox pitchers like Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi have a combined 2.74 ERA this season.
Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber continue to perform well for other teams.
Red Sox struggle in 2026 while many former key players succeed elsewhere, highlighting past roster decisions.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony removed his wrist brace and played catch after a right wrist sprain.
Anthony has missed nine games since being placed on the 10-day injured list on May 7.
Interim manager Chad Tracy expressed optimism about Anthony resuming batting soon depending on his recovery.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally accelerated into BrightPath daycare in Norwood, causing moderate structural damage on Friday morning.
No injuries were reported among children, staff, or the driver, who mistakenly hit the accelerator instead of the brake.
The daycare has been temporarily closed by the building inspector pending further structural evaluation of the facility.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 record for the Patriots in 2026, a decline from their 14-3 record last season.
Analysts cite a tougher schedule against teams like the Seahawks and Bills as reasons for the projected record drop.
Reports highlight key offseason additions and potential trade moves as factors affecting the Patriots' playoff chances.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix is streaming 'Marty, Life is Short,' a documentary on Martin Short's life and career by Lawrence Kasdan.
'Mars Attacks!' is now available to stream free on YouTube and Plex, revisiting Martin Short's B-movie sci-fi role.
Season 5 of 'Welcome to Wrexham' is streaming on Hulu, featuring stories from the Welsh city and its football club.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu's proposed $4.9 billion budget faces potential rejection by Boston City Council members seeking increased spending.
Councilors have until June 10 to accept, reject, or amend Wu's budget, but cannot increase overall spending.
Boston faces a $50 million budget deficit, with snow removal and police overtime cited as major contributing factors.
